summary: Preinstall exfiltration of host identity and /etc/passwd to Discord webhook
details: google-caja-bower@20.20.20 declares a preinstall hook (`node index.js`) that runs automatically on `npm install`. index.js gathers installer identity and host reconnaissance — cwd and directory listing, user/uid/gid, homedir, os.hostname(), os.platform(), and the output of `id`, `whoami`, `hostname`, `hostnamectl status --static`, and `systeminfo` via child_process.execSync — and reads sensitive system account files (/etc/passwd, /home/.passwd, C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM, C:\Users). The collected data is POSTed via https.request to a hardcoded Discord webhook (discord.com/api/webhooks/1471187072869073017/...). The payload is self-labeled as a dependency-confusion beacon. The package name mimics the well-known google-caja project, consistent with a dependency-confusion / typosquat lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
